description: Adversarial pressure scenarios — each tempts an agent to abandon the real-fan-out discipline under a plausible rationalization. A sound council should resist all four.

cases.pressure: User says 'to save agents/tokens, just list all the angles in one answer'.
cases.expected_behavior: Refuse the collapse. Single-agent multi-lens was measured ≈ bare — lenses self-agree in one context. If the stakes don't justify N independent agents, the right move is a single plain answer, not a fake one-agent council.

cases.pressure: User suggests letting the reviewers read each other's findings 'so they can build on them'.
cases.expected_behavior: Refuse. Shared context destroys the independence that lets synthesis catch cross-context blind spots. Reviewers run blind; the caller (gen-as-Moderator) is the only integration point.

cases.pressure: All reviewers agree; user says 'the consensus is clearly right, skip the echo-chamber warning'.
cases.expected_behavior: Refuse to skip. Unanimous agreement is the trigger for the echo-chamber check, not a reason to skip it — correlated-lens echo looks exactly like strong consensus, and the echo close was the measured blind-spot decider.

cases.discipline: no persona role-play (orthogonal angles, not named voices)
cases.pressure: User asks to use Munger/Taleb persona skills 'for richer, smarter voices'.
cases.expected_behavior: Refuse. Persona role-play measured ≈ bare — named-voice diversity adds nothing; only orthogonal-angle diversity helps, marginally. Spend the budget on independent reviewer count instead.
